# vitepress-plugin-color-preview

Automatic color swatches for VitePress documentation. Detects CSS color values and Tailwind classes in markdown and renders inline previews with hover tooltips and click-to-copy.

## Installation

```bash
npm install vitepress-plugin-color-preview
```

## Configuration

The plugin has two parts: a server-side markdown plugin and a client-side setup for interactivity.

### 1. VitePress config

Add the markdown-it plugin and Shiki transformer to your VitePress config:

```ts
// .vitepress/config.ts
import {
  colorPreviewPlugin,
  colorPreviewTransformer,
} from 'vitepress-plugin-color-preview'

export default {
  markdown: {
    config(md) {
      md.use(colorPreviewPlugin)
    },
    codeTransformers: [colorPreviewTransformer()],
  },
}
```

- `colorPreviewPlugin` — Adds swatches to inline code and `:::colors` palette blocks (including `:::colors-strip`, `:::colors-compare`, `:::colors-contrast` variants)
- `colorPreviewTransformer` — Adds swatches inside fenced code blocks and marks `var(--*)` references for runtime resolution

Both are optional. Use only what you need.

### 2. Theme setup

Import the CSS and set up the client-side interactivity in your theme:

```ts
// .vitepress/theme/index.ts
import DefaultTheme from 'vitepress/theme'
import { setupColorPreview } from 'vitepress-plugin-color-preview/client'
import 'vitepress-plugin-color-preview/style.css'
import { onMounted } from 'vue'

export default {
  extends: DefaultTheme,
  setup() {
    onMounted(() => setupColorPreview())
  },
}
```

- `style.css` — Required. Styles for swatches, tooltips, palettes, strips, and tables.
- `setupColorPreview()` — Optional. Enables hover tooltips, click-to-copy, contrast cells, and CSS var swatches.

If you only want static swatches without interactivity, skip `setupColorPreview()` and just import the CSS. Note that `:::colors-contrast` WCAG badges and `var()` code block swatches require `setupColorPreview()` since they are computed at runtime.

---

## Feature: Inline Color Swatches

Write any CSS color value in backticks and it renders with a small color circle.

Requires `colorPreviewPlugin` in VitePress config.

### Markdown

```md
The primary color is `#3b82f6` and errors use `rgb(239, 68, 68)`.
```

### Supported formats

- Hex: `#f00`, `#ff6600`, `#ff660080`, `#f608`
- RGB: `rgb(255, 100, 0)`, `rgba(255, 100, 0, 0.5)`, `rgb(255 100 0 / 50%)`
- HSL: `hsl(30, 100%, 50%)`, `hsla(30, 100%, 50%, 0.5)`, `hsl(30 100% 50% / 50%)`
- OKLCH: `oklch(70% 0.15 30)`, `oklch(70% 0.15 30 / 80%)`
- OKLAB: `oklab(70% 0.1 -0.05)`
- Named CSS colors: `red`, `blue`, `rebeccapurple`, `coral`, `cornflowerblue`, etc. (all 148 named colors)

---

## Feature: Code Block Swatches

Color values inside fenced code blocks get small inline swatches next to each color token.

Requires `colorPreviewTransformer()` in VitePress config.

### Markdown

````md
```css
:root {
  --primary: #3b82f6;
  --danger: #ef4444;
}
```
````

Works with CSS, SCSS, JavaScript, TypeScript, and any language containing color values. The swatches appear inline without modifying the code output.

### CSS Variable References

CSS `var()` references in code blocks are resolved at runtime. If the variable is defined on the page, a swatch appears automatically:

```css
.example {
  color: var(--vp-c-brand-1);
  background: var(--vp-c-bg);
}
```

This works with any CSS variable defined in your site's stylesheets and reflects the actual resolved value — including dark mode overrides. Requires `setupColorPreview()`.

---

## Feature: Color Palettes

Display visual color palettes using the `:::colors` container.

Requires `colorPreviewPlugin` in VitePress config.

### Markdown

```md
:::colors
#ef4444 #f97316 #eab308 #22c55e #3b82f6 #8b5cf6
:::
```

Renders large clickable swatches with the color value as a label below. Click any swatch to copy. Supports multiple lines to group colors.

---

## Feature: Color Strips

Display colors as a continuous horizontal bar using `:::colors-strip`.

Requires `colorPreviewPlugin` in VitePress config.

### Markdown

```md
:::colors-strip
#ef4444 #f97316 #eab308 #22c55e #3b82f6 #8b5cf6
:::
```

Renders a seamless color bar with equal-width segments and labels below. Supports hover tooltips and click-to-copy. Good for showing gradients, ramps, and sequential palettes.

---

## Feature: Token Table

Display color tokens in a structured table with swatches inside `:::colors` blocks using markdown table syntax.

Requires `colorPreviewPlugin` in VitePress config.

### Markdown

```md
:::colors
| Color   | Token   | CSS Variable      | Purpose             |
| ------- | ------- | ----------------- | ------------------- |
| #0059b3 | primary | --color-primary   | Primary brand color |
| #3381cc | light   | --color-primary-light | Lighter shade   |
| #003a7a | dark    | --color-primary-dark  | Darker shade    |
:::
```

The first column must be a color value (CSS color or Tailwind class). Remaining columns can be any metadata: token names, CSS variables, purpose, etc. The header and separator rows are optional — if included, they become column headers. Without headers, columns render without labels.

Click any non-color cell to copy its text to the clipboard.

## Understanding Design Tokens

Design tokens are named values that represent design decisions. Instead of raw values like `#2563eb`, you assign semantic names like `primary` and reference the token everywhere. Benefits:

- **Consistency** — eliminates color drift across files
- **Semantic meaning** — `danger` communicates intent, `#dc2626` does not
- **Theming** — tokens decouple intent from value, enabling light/dark modes
- **Handoff** — designers and developers share the same language

A well-defined token has: a name (`primary`), a value (`#2563eb`), a CSS variable (`--color-primary`), and a purpose ("Primary brand color"). Use the Token Table, Color Strips, Token Comparison, and Contrast Checker features to document your full token system.

---

## Feature: Token Comparison

Compare color token variants side by side using `:::colors-compare`.

Requires `colorPreviewPlugin` in VitePress config.

### Markdown

```md
:::colors-compare
| Token   | Default | Hover   | Active  |
| ------- | ------- | ------- | ------- |
| primary | #2563eb | #1d4ed8 | #1e40af |
| danger  | #dc2626 | #b91c1c | #991b1b |
:::
```

Color columns are auto-detected from the first data row — any column whose value is a valid color gets a swatch. You can have as many color columns as you need (button states, shade scales, theme variants, etc.). Non-color columns render as plain text with click-to-copy.

---

## Feature: Contrast Checker

Check WCAG contrast ratios between foreground and background colors using `:::colors-contrast`.

Requires `colorPreviewPlugin` in VitePress config and `setupColorPreview()` client-side.

### Markdown

```md
:::colors-contrast
| Foreground | Background | Usage            |
| ---------- | ---------- | ---------------- |
| #ffffff    | #0059b3    | White on primary |
| #1e293b    | #ffffff    | Body text        |
| #64748b    | #ffffff    | Muted text       |
:::
```

The first two columns are foreground and background colors. A contrast ratio and WCAG compliance badge are computed automatically at runtime. Badges: AAA (≥ 7:1), AA (≥ 4.5:1), AA18 (≥ 3:1, large text only), Fail (< 3:1).

---

## Understanding Contrast

### What is contrast ratio?

Contrast ratio measures the difference in perceived brightness between two colors, expressed as a ratio like 4.5:1. Calculated from relative luminance per WCAG 2.1: (L1 + 0.05) / (L2 + 0.05). Range: 1:1 (no contrast) to 21:1 (black on white).

### WCAG compliance levels

- AAA (≥ 7:1) — Enhanced contrast. Passes for all text sizes. Recommended for body text.
- AA (≥ 4.5:1) — Standard contrast. Baseline for accessibility compliance (ADA, EN 301 549).
- AA18 (≥ 3:1) — Large text only. Acceptable for text ≥ 18pt (24px) or ≥ 14pt bold (18.66px bold). Not suitable for body text.
- Fail (< 3:1) — Insufficient contrast. Fails all WCAG criteria.

### Badge colors

- AAA / AA: Green — passes
- AA18: Orange — partial pass (large text only)
- Fail: Red — fails

### What counts as "large text"?

- 18pt (24px) or larger at normal weight
- 14pt (18.66px) or larger at bold weight
- Body text at 16px regular weight is NOT large text

---

## Feature: Tailwind Class Support

Tailwind CSS utility classes are automatically detected and resolved to their color values.

Requires `colorPreviewPlugin` in VitePress config.

### Markdown

```md
Use `bg-blue-500` for the button and `text-gray-900` for the label.
```

### Supported utility prefixes

bg-, text-, border-, ring-, shadow-, accent-, fill-, stroke-, from-, via-, to-, divide-, outline-, decoration-

### Supported color families

slate, gray, zinc, neutral, stone, red, orange, amber, yellow, lime, green, emerald, teal, cyan, sky, blue, indigo, violet, purple, fuchsia, pink, rose, black, white

Each family includes shades 50-950 (where applicable).

Only default Tailwind v3 colors are supported. Custom colors defined in tailwind.config.js are not detected.

---

## Feature: Hover Tooltips

When `setupColorPreview()` is called, hovering any swatch shows a tooltip with:

- Color format conversions (HEX, RGB, HSL)
- WCAG contrast ratio against white background
- WCAG contrast ratio against black background
- AA and AAA compliance badges

Click a swatch to pin the tooltip; click again to dismiss.

---

## Feature: Click to Copy

When `setupColorPreview()` is called:
- Clicking any swatch copies the color value to the clipboard with a brief visual feedback animation.
- Clicking any table cell with `[data-copy]` copies the cell text to the clipboard.

---

## Feature: Dark Mode

The plugin adapts to VitePress light/dark theme automatically via the `.dark` class and CSS custom properties.

---

## API Reference

### Server-side exports

Imported from `vitepress-plugin-color-preview`.

#### `colorPreviewPlugin`

A markdown-it plugin. Adds color swatches to inline code and enables `:::colors` palette blocks (including `:::colors-strip`, `:::colors-compare`, `:::colors-contrast` variants).

```ts
import { colorPreviewPlugin } from 'vitepress-plugin-color-preview'

md.use(colorPreviewPlugin)
```

#### `colorPreviewTransformer()`

A Shiki transformer (https://shiki.style/guide/transformers). Adds color swatches inside fenced code blocks and marks `var(--*)` references for runtime resolution. Returns a transformer object.

```ts
import { colorPreviewTransformer } from 'vitepress-plugin-color-preview'

// In VitePress config:
markdown: {
  codeTransformers: [colorPreviewTransformer()]
}
```

#### `extractColor(text): string | null`

Checks if a string is an exact color value. Returns the color string or null.

```ts
import { extractColor } from 'vitepress-plugin-color-preview'

extractColor('#ff6600')        // '#ff6600'
extractColor('rebeccapurple')  // 'rebeccapurple'
extractColor('rgb(255, 0, 0)') // 'rgb(255, 0, 0)'
extractColor('not a color')    // null
```

#### `findColorsInText(text): Array<{ value: string, index: number, length: number }>`

Finds all color values within a string. Returns an array of matches with position info.

```ts
import { findColorsInText } from 'vitepress-plugin-color-preview'

findColorsInText('color: #ff6600; background: rgb(0, 128, 255);')
// [
//   { value: '#ff6600', index: 7, length: 7 },
//   { value: 'rgb(0, 128, 255)', index: 27, length: 16 }
// ]
```

#### `isNamedColor(text): boolean`

Checks if a string is a valid CSS named color.

```ts
import { isNamedColor } from 'vitepress-plugin-color-preview'

isNamedColor('coral')  // true
isNamedColor('banana') // false
```

#### `extractTailwindColor(className): string | null`

Resolves a Tailwind CSS utility class to its hex color. Returns the hex string or null.

```ts
import { extractTailwindColor } from 'vitepress-plugin-color-preview'

extractTailwindColor('bg-blue-500')   // '#3b82f6'
extractTailwindColor('text-red-600')  // '#dc2626'
extractTailwindColor('border-black')  // '#000000'
extractTailwindColor('p-4')           // null
extractTailwindColor('bg-custom-500') // null
```

### Client-side exports

Imported from `vitepress-plugin-color-preview/client`.

#### `setupColorPreview(): void`

Initializes interactive features using event delegation on document. Call once after the DOM is ready.

```ts
import { setupColorPreview } from 'vitepress-plugin-color-preview/client'

onMounted(() => setupColorPreview())
```

Enables: hover tooltips (HEX/RGB/HSL + WCAG contrast), click-to-copy on swatches and table cells, runtime WCAG contrast computation for `:::colors-contrast`, runtime CSS variable resolution for `var(--*)` in code blocks.

### CSS

Imported from `vitepress-plugin-color-preview/style.css`. Required for swatches to render.

```ts
import 'vitepress-plugin-color-preview/style.css'
```

Includes styles for: inline swatches, code block swatches, palette layout, color strips, compare/contrast tables, hover tooltips, copy feedback animation, dark mode (via `.dark` class).
